2606.12068.title: StanceNakba Shared Task: Actor and Topic-Aware Stance Detection in Public Discourse
2606.12068.paper_summary: We present StanceNakba 2026, a shared task on stance detection in polarized social media discourse related to the Palestinian-Israeli conflict, organized as part of Nakba-NLP 2026 at LREC-COLING 2026. The task introduces two subtasks: Subtask A (Actor-Level Stance Detection), which classifies English social media posts as Pro-Palestine, Pro-Israel, or Neutral; and Subtask B (Cross-Topic Stance Detection), which identifies Favor, Against, or Neither stances in Arabic posts toward two conflict-related topics, normalization with Israel and refugee presence in Jordan. The task is grounded in an annotated dataset of 2,606 social media posts. A total of 7 teams participated in Subtask A and 6 teams in Subtask B. Participating systems primarily fine-tuned Arabic and multilingual transformer-based models, including MARBERT, AraBERT, and DeBERTa-v3 variants, with several teams employing cross-validation, ensemble methods, and topic-conditioned architectures. The best-performing systems achieved a Macro F1 of 0.9620 on Subtask A and 0.8724 on Subtask B, demonstrating that transformer-based approaches are highly effective for conflict-domain stance detection while highlighting persistent challenges in cross-topic generalization and neutral class prediction.
2606.12068.paper_summary_zh: 我們呈現 StanceNakba 2026，一個針對與巴勒斯坦-以色列衝突相關的極化社交媒體話語中的立場檢測的共享任務，作為 Nakba-NLP 2026 在 LREC-COLING 2026 的一部分。該任務介紹了兩個子任務：子任務 A（參與者級別立場檢測），將英文社交媒體帖子分類為支持巴勒斯坦、支持以色列或中立；以及子任務 B（跨主題立場檢測），識別阿拉伯語帖子對於兩個與衝突相關主題的立場，即對以色列的正常化和約旦的難民存在的支持、反對或不偏不倚。該任務基於一個標註的數據集，包含 2,606 條社交媒體帖子。共有 7 支隊伍參加了子任務 A，6 支隊伍參加了子任務 B。參賽系統主要對阿拉伯語和多語言的Transformer模型進行了微調，包括 MARBERT、AraBERT 和 DeBERTa-v3 變體，幾支隊伍還採用了交叉驗證、集成方法和主題條件架構。表現最佳的系統在子任務 A 上達到了 0.9620 的宏觀 F1 分數，在子任務 B 上達到了 0.8724，顯示出基於Transformer的方法在衝突領域的立場檢測中非常有效，同時突顯了在跨主題泛化和中立類別預測中持續存在的挑戰。
2606.12068.author: Kholoud K. Aldous et.al.
2606.12068.authors: Kholoud K. Aldous, Md Rafiul Biswas, Mabrouka Bessghaier, Shimaa Ibrahim, Kais Attia, Wajdi Zaghouani
